commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider capability methods for lease defaults, finalization, release cleanup, and image operations, rewires Worker fleet routes to call them, and updates provider-boundary docs/tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor PR rather than a bug report. Behavioral preservation is reviewable through the merge diff, added Worker tests, and the PR’s live AWS proof.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; maintainers should decide whether the current tests and AWS live proof are enough for the provider-lifecycle refactor.

target.title: refactor(worker): route provider behavior through capabilities
target.body:
## Summary

- route provider-owned lease defaults, post-provision metadata, release cleanup, native images, AWS image promotion, and FSR handling through provider capabilities
- keep fleet core focused on orchestration, authorization, cost limits, persistence, and provider capability dispatch
- make provider clients lazy where capability checks should not require provider credentials
- document the provider boundary in AGENTS and vision docs
- add regression coverage for promoted AWS image deletion, unsupported native-image providers, provider release/default/finalization behavior, and existing AWS SSH access reconciliation paths

## Verification

- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `npm run docs:check`
- `npm run check`
- `go test ./...`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- live AWS Crabbox proof: provider=`aws`, lease=`cbx_61381b273f3e`, run=`run_e026ec0eb84d`, `npm ci --prefix worker && npm run check --prefix worker && npm test --prefix worker -- fleet.test.ts aws.test.ts`, exit=0, leaseStopped=true

## Audit Notes

Remaining provider-name branches are routing/config/admin gates rather than resource policy. The next cleanup candidates are direct CLI image/checkpoint switches in `internal/cli/checkpoint_native.go` and direct stop/delete fallback routing in `internal/cli/run.go`; those should move behind CLI provider backend capabilities if they grow further.
target.author: steipete
